title: eriogonum scabrellum
intro: Eriogonum scabrellum, also known as desert buckwheat or desert fireweed, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Eriogonum scabrellum have been found to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection, making it beneficial for treating minor cuts and scrapes. Eriogonum scabrellum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
